name: Summer Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. Halve the strawberries and remove the yellow zest from the lemon, leaving the white pith behind.
2. In a frying pan, combine the berries, sugar, nutmeg, lemon zest, and 1/3 cup water.
3. Cook over medium heat until the fruit is soft but still intact, about 8-10 minutes.
4. Remove from heat and let cool. Discard the lemon zest and reserve the juice.
5. Drain the fruit, reserving any juices.
6. Line a 5-cup bowl with plastic wrap.
7. Trim the crusts from the bread slices.
8. Dip one side of each bread slice in the reserved juice and line the bottom and sides of the prepared bowl, moistened side toward the bowl.
9. Cut the bread as needed to fit into the bowl.
10. Pour in the fruit mixture. Top with remaining bread, trimming if necessary.
11. Brush any remaining juice over the top of the bread.
12. Cover with plastic wrap and place a plate on top that fits into the bowl.
13. Add a weight, such as a 28-ounce can, and refrigerate overnight.
14. Turn the pudding out onto a serving plate, cut into wedges, and serve with whipped cream if desired.
